title: Elai Kaatha Amman Temple
tagline: Elai Kaatha Amman Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Elai Kaatha Amman Temple, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Ezhai Kaatha Amman Temple (Tamil: ஏழை காத்த அம்மன் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple situated in the Madurai district of Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Ezhai Kaatha Amman Temple (Tamil: ஏழை காத்த அம்மன் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple situated in the Madurai district of Tamil Nadu, India. This temple is exactly located in a place called "Koyil Patti", within the Panchayat limit of Kurichipatti. Koyil Patti is approximately 14 km from Melur. Elai Kaatha Amman Temple comes under the place called Vellalur which is called by people as Vellalur Nadu (Nadu means country).

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Vellaloor massacre
topPlaces.description: The Vellaloor massacre refers to the killing of nearly 5000 people of the Kallar clan at the Vellalore village near Melur Taluk in what is now the Madurai District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The massacre was ordered by the Nawab of the Carnatic to a "Captain Rumley" in charge of his army.

topPlaces.name: Thaniyamangalam
topPlaces.description: Thaniyamangalam is a village in the Melur taluk of Madurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. The sand is reddish due to rich iron.

topPlaces.name: Mallakottai
topPlaces.description: Mallakottai is the name of a panchayat village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It was one of the villages of the ancient "Mallakottai Nadu" once, which was a part of 'Sivagangai Seemai'..

topPlaces.name: Jeyankondanilai
topPlaces.description: Jeyankondanilai is a head village in singampunari taluk, Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village is predominantly Kallar village with a minor section of SC community and a couple of families of other castes.

topPlaces.name: Keelappoongudi
topPlaces.description: Keelappoongudi is a panchayat Village in Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village is under the control of Sivaganga block.

items.note: The Vellaloor massacre refers to the killing of nearly 5000 people of the Kallar clan at the Vellalore village near Melur Taluk in what is now the Madurai District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The massacre was ordered by the Nawab of the Carnatic to a "Captain Rumley" in charge of his army.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Elai Kaatha Amman Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Elai Kaatha Amman Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Elai Kaatha Amman Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (33 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Elai Kaatha Amman Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Elai Kaatha Amman Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Elai Kaatha Amman Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Elai Kaatha Amman Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Elai Kaatha Amman Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vellaloor massacre, Thaniyamangalam, Mallakottai, Jeyankondanilai, Keelappoongudi.
